business_ideas.idea: First business doing $10M in revenue per year (specific vertical not fully detailed in sample)
Second business doing $20M in revenue per year (specific vertical not fully detailed in sample)
Third business doing $30M in revenue per year (specific vertical not fully detailed in sample)

dynamics: Hosts discuss three unusual businesses that perform at specific revenue tiers ($10M, $20M, $30M). Episode structure around sharing discoveries from outside their typical cone of vision. Shaan and Sam appear to be in-person, casual conversational format celebrating unexpected business models; high energy around finding hidden opportunities.
frameworks: Boring/weird business discovery: identifying profitable models operating outside startup/venture hype cycles
Revenue-tier focus: analyzing what works at specific scale points rather than hypergrowth/unicorn narratives
learnings: Profitable, sustainable businesses exist outside venture-backed narratives and typically receive no media attention
Business models at $10M-$30M ARR tier represent underexplored opportunity categories for entrepreneurs
Searching outside typical startup circles reveals entirely different business logic and success factors

banger_quotes.quote: Those are three ideas that didn't even exist in my cone of vision
We get high high and low low equal joy from bodega pops or hanging with the 80th richest person

notable: Hosts celebrate three unusual/overlooked profitable business models outside typical media attention; frame is about discovering non-obvious opportunities rather than building new ones.
